title: iface_link_addr copy could use sizeof instead of constant
description:
lichen_l2.c:513 uses:
```c
memcpy(iface_link_addr, eui64, LICHEN_L2_ADDR_LEN);
```

But iface_link_addr is declared as:
```c
static uint8_t iface_link_addr[LICHEN_L2_ADDR_LEN];
```

What goes wrong: If someone changes the array size but not the memcpy, silent truncation or buffer overread occurs.

Rewrite:
```c
memcpy(iface_link_addr, eui64, sizeof(iface_link_addr));
```

This ties the copy size to the actual buffer, preventing mismatch.
